note: Existing off-scale CSS values. The checker fails only for new IDs not listed here.

violations.id: app/globals.css|pill" recipes beyond this one — .rl-pill (run-sheet detail tag, 24px min, 6px radius, left color-bar), .matkit__pill (kit-coverage badge, --r-sm not --r-pill — a rounded-rect, not a true pill), .cal-kit-chip (calendar day icon-button, no border), .book-print-chip (36px-tall clickable control sized to match its header siblings), .admin-status (static per-state badge, no min-height/cursor), and .theme-badge (swatch + label, no border/background at all). Each was checked for a safe merge into this shared .chip base and rejected: every one carries a DELIBERATE size, radius, or interaction difference (touch target, click vs static, or genuinely non-pill shape) that a shared class would either break or require per-site overrides to preserve — i.e. fewer than 3 were byte-for-byte redundant, so consolidating further risked a visible regression for no real de-duplication win. Left as-is; revisit only if a recipe's own job changes. */ .chip|font-size|11px|screen
violations.selector: pill" recipes beyond this one — .rl-pill (run-sheet detail tag, 24px min, 6px radius, left color-bar), .matkit__pill (kit-coverage badge, --r-sm not --r-pill — a rounded-rect, not a true pill), .cal-kit-chip (calendar day icon-button, no border), .book-print-chip (36px-tall clickable control sized to match its header siblings), .admin-status (static per-state badge, no min-height/cursor), and .theme-badge (swatch + label, no border/background at all). Each was checked for a safe merge into this shared .chip base and rejected: every one carries a DELIBERATE size, radius, or interaction difference (touch target, click vs static, or genuinely non-pill shape) that a shared class would either break or require per-site overrides to preserve — i.e. fewer than 3 were byte-for-byte redundant, so consolidating further risked a visible regression for no real de-duplication win. Left as-is; revisit only if a recipe's own job changes. */ .chip

violations.id: app/globals.css|background-image: linear-gradient(var(--ink) 1px, transparent 1px), linear-gradient(90deg, var(--ink) 1px, transparent 1px); .plate__mono|font-size|56px|screen
violations.selector: background-image: linear-gradient(var(--ink) 1px, transparent 1px), linear-gradient(90deg, var(--ink) 1px, transparent 1px); .plate__mono

violations.id: app/globals.css|ballooning to fill the gap. `--pb-cols` (set inline from the frame count, capped at 3) fixes the column basis so the track size is identical on every row, and auto-fill drops columns as the container narrows. */ .rl-diagram .playbook--compact .playbook__frames|gap|16px 14px|screen
violations.selector: ballooning to fill the gap. `--pb-cols` (set inline from the frame count, capped at 3) fixes the column basis so the track size is identical on every row, and auto-fill drops columns as the container narrows. */ .rl-diagram .playbook--compact .playbook__frames

violations.id: app/calendar.css|/* Base 15-min slot height, scaled by the pinch-to-zoom factor (--cal-slot-zoom, default 1; set on .calshell__grid by CalendarShell). Pinch out → taller hours for fine detail; pinch in → denser hours for a whole-day overview. The vertical analogue of the Day/Week/N horizontal zoom. */ /* Let the slot rows compress all the way down when pinched OUT. An empty table cell otherwise keeps a full line-box (~the inherited font's line height), which floors the row height — so without this, pinch-OUT does nothing. We zero the line box on the (text-less) lane + empty axis cells so the calc() height above wins down to its minimum; the hour-label cushion re-asserts a normal line-box so the time text stays readable (giving the hour rows a gentle floor — they simply read as the slightly roomier hour ticks at the densest zoom). */ .calshell__grid--strip .fc-timegrid-slot-lane|font-size|0|screen
violations.selector: /* Base 15-min slot height, scaled by the pinch-to-zoom factor (--cal-slot-zoom, default 1; set on .calshell__grid by CalendarShell). Pinch out → taller hours for fine detail; pinch in → denser hours for a whole-day overview. The vertical analogue of the Day/Week/N horizontal zoom. */ /* Let the slot rows compress all the way down when pinched OUT. An empty table cell otherwise keeps a full line-box (~the inherited font's line height), which floors the row height — so without this, pinch-OUT does nothing. We zero the line box on the (text-less) lane + empty axis cells so the calc() height above wins down to its minimum; the hour-label cushion re-asserts a normal line-box so the time text stays readable (giving the hour rows a gentle floor — they simply read as the slightly roomier hour ticks at the densest zoom). */ .calshell__grid--strip .fc-timegrid-slot-lane

violations.id: app/calendar.css|labels so an overlapped label (e.g. 12 PM when it's 11:55) is cleanly hidden, never doubled — that's the axis-col z-index lift in the strip block below. */ .calshell .fc .fc-timegrid-now-indicator-arrow|padding|2px var(--s-2)|screen
violations.selector: labels so an overlapped label (e.g. 12 PM when it's 11:55) is cleanly hidden, never doubled — that's the axis-col z-index lift in the strip block below. */ .calshell .fc .fc-timegrid-now-indicator-arrow

violations.selector: branch bails on altKey), so the copy "+" badge (body.is-cal-copy …::after, same pseudo) should take over rather than the count pill. */ body:not(.is-cal-copy) .cal-dragfollow.is-group::after

violations.id: app/calendar.css|branch bails on altKey), so the copy "+" badge (body.is-cal-copy …::after, same pseudo) should take over rather than the count pill. */ body:not(.is-cal-copy) .cal-dragfollow.is-group::after|box-shadow|0 0 0 1.5px var(--card)|screen
violations.selector: branch bails on altKey), so the copy "+" badge (body.is-cal-copy …::after, same pseudo) should take over rather than the count pill. */ body:not(.is-cal-copy) .cal-dragfollow.is-group::after

violations.id: app/calendar.css|/* A faint paper backdrop so the mark stays legible over a coloured event card, without becoming a hard pill. */ transition: color var(--t-fast) var(--ease), background-color var(--t-fast) var(--ease), transform var(--t-fast) var(--ease); .cal-wx-chip .cal-wx-glyph|font-size|12px|screen
